campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The City Council heard a financial update from the local transportation authority about their March 2026 investments and spending. They also voted on a new policy that prevents the city from allowing federal immigration enforcement activities on city property, and discussed ways to attract and keep city employees.
keyTopics: Transportation Authority's March 2026 money report
New policy blocking federal immigration enforcement on city property
City job vacancies and efforts to recruit and retain workers

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a meeting to address dangerous or damaged properties that need to be cleaned up or fixed, allowing residents to voice concerns about the county's list. The council approved a new policy on how the city will handle technology changes and established a process to help residents who need special accommodations when dealing with city services.
keyTopics: Public hearing on removing hazards from neglected properties
New technology disruption policy for city operations
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations from the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve several routine matters including minutes from their previous meeting and agreements for services. The council also addressed a hazardous vegetation problem by officially declaring overgrown brush a public nuisance and approving plans for landscape and lighting improvements in the area.
keyTopics: Hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation declared a public nuisance
Landscape and lighting improvements approved
Five-year service agreements authorized with Pacific (company name incomplete)
Previous meeting minutes approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to review and finalize their upcoming agenda for May 19, 2026. They discussed adding new items to the agenda, scheduling specific times for certain topics, and approved the meeting schedule for the next fiscal year (2026-2027). The council also handled appointment decisions for various city committees and boards.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 19, 2026 City Council agenda
Approved the 2026-2027 fiscal year meeting schedule
Made appointments to city committees, boards, and commissions
Reviewed travel requests from council members

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 12, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a private meeting to discuss two main issues: a water main problem at Cuesta Park that might lead to a lawsuit, and negotiations to buy or lease two properties on Clyde Avenue. These closed-door discussions involved city lawyers and real estate staff working through potential legal and financial matters.
keyTopics: Water main damage at Cuesta Park and possible lawsuit
City negotiating to acquire or lease properties at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue
Legal strategy discussion with city attorney
Real estate deal terms and property valuation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. The meeting included a flag salute and appears to have included a special order focused on small business topics, though specific details from the closed session were not publicly disclosed.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not public)
Small business special order
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
Limited agenda details provided

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held an initial planning meeting to discuss Cupertino's budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The council also reminded the public about rules requiring people who lobby city officials to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and planning
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Rules for those attempting to influence city legislative or administrative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held an Economic Development & Marketing Committee meeting on May 11, 2026. The city is continuing to offer both in-person and remote options for residents to participate and share their views on economic development and marketing initiatives.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee (Ad Hoc) meeting held
In-person and remote participation options available (Zoom, phone)
Public comment submission deadline: 11 AM day of meeting via email

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to plan the city's finances and development for the next several years. They approved a five-year spending plan for major projects, reviewed the upcoming year's budget, and adopted a master plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District.
keyTopics: Approved the 5-year Capital Improvement Plan (2027-2031) for city infrastructure projects
Reviewed the FY 2026-27 Operating Budget proposal
Adopted the Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council meeting procedures and handbook
Approved previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing the city parking lot there. All 72 apartments would be permanently affordable for low-income residents, and the developer is requesting special allowances under state law to make the project work on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units proposed for 450 Lytton Avenue
Building would replace 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Six-story development on 0.43-acre parcel
Request for density bonus concessions under state law
100% affordable housing project
